title: 帷幕
summary:
In a letter to Hastings, Hercule Poirot reveals that he has been feigning paralysis at Stiles Manor to deceive Curtis. Poirot explains that he sent George away and replaced him with less experienced staff because George’s astute judgment would have discovered that Poirot’s health had actually improved after his return from Egypt. Poirot admits he witnessed Hastings attempting to kill Alden by tampering with his sleeping pills. To prevent the crime, Poirot observed Hastings through a bathroom keyhole, then used his own sleeping pills to ensure Hastings fell asleep after drinking hot chocolate, thereby preventing a murder.

Poirot expresses concern that Norton’s schemes may eventually target Hastings' daughter. He then reveals the truth regarding Barbara Franklin's death, asserting that Hastings was the unwitting killer. Poirot explains that Mrs. Franklin was an ambitious woman who desired to marry the wealthy Boyd Carrington. To achieve this, she planned to have her husband, John Franklin, die of poisoned aconite under the guise of a scientific experiment. However, Barbara felt threatened by the social dynamics involving Crawford nurse and decided to act prematurely to become a widow.

During a moment when the household went outside to view a shooting star, Mrs. Franklin prepared coffee using a rotating table. While Hastings remained behind to work on a crossword puzzle, the coffee was switched: Mrs. Franklin accidentally consumed the strychnine intended for John, while John drank the coffee intended for her. Poirot admits that although he realized the truth, he allowed the death to be framed as a suicide to protect the innocence of John Franklin and Judith.
